arguments.command:
# Download and check BuildIds for several .NET 10 versions
# The crash timestamp is 2026-02-25, so let's focus on versions likely current then
# .NET 10 was released Nov 2025, so early 2026 would be 10.0.1, 10.0.2, maybe 10.0.3

mkdir -p /tmp/nupkg

for ver in 10.0.1 10.0.2 10.0.3 10.0.0; do
  echo "=== Checking $ver ==="
  curl -sSL -o "/tmp/nupkg/runtime-${ver}.nupkg" \
    "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64/${ver}/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64.${ver}.nupkg" \
    -w "HTTP: %{http_code}, Size: %{size_download}\n"
  
  # Extract just the libcoreclr.so and check BuildId
  cd /tmp/nupkg
  unzip -o -q "runtime-${ver}.nupkg" "runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" 2>/dev/null
  if [ -f runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so ]; then
    readelf -n runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so 2>/dev/null | grep "Build ID"
    rm -f runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so
  else
    echo "libcoreclr.so not found in package"
    # Check what's in the package
    unzip -l "runtime-${ver}.nupkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -i coreclr | head -3
  fi
  rm -f "runtime-${ver}.nupkg"
  cd /tmp/sv-c28e661520dc40b5bfdb10292120106c
  echo
done
